sections.paragraph:
ἀνίατος, ον (α-priv., ἰάομαι ‘to heal’; Hippocr., Pla. et al.; IG III add. 171a., 23 p. 474 of an ‘incurable’ sore healed by Asclepius; pap, LXX; TestSol 18:20, 23; Jos., Ant. 19, 325 [adv.]; Just., D. 132, 2) incurable only fig. (Aeschines 3, 156 κακά; TestReub 6:3) ἁμαρτία μεγάλη καὶ ἀ. a great and unforgivable sin Hm 5, 2, 4 (cp. Philo, Somn. 1, 87 τὰ ἀνίατα τ. ἁμαρτημάτων; Pla., Gorg. 525c διὰ τοιαῦτα ἀδικήματα ἀνίατοι).—DELG s.v. ἰάομαι.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

add. add. = additional

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 82
